addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Novel BUK mechanism plus the first feeder. The design choice is settled: a shared RBK-ignorant per-pair emit primitive, colophons landing in the `RBTDGC_` file. Additive, low-risk.
  
  ## Goal
  
  Build the shared bash→Rust const-emit primitive and wire the zipper's colophons as its first feeder, landing them in the generated `RBTDGC_` file. The primitive names nothing domain-specific; colophons are the proving case.
  
  ## Shape
  
  - `buz_enroll` must retain the enroll varname in a new co-indexed roll — today it is `printf -v`'d into the caller and discarded, and it is the source of the const stem. Additive: `printf -v` and the existing rolls stay, callers untouched.
  - Add an RBK-ignorant BUK primitive that formats one `pub const NAME: &str = "VALUE";` line from a name and value, modeled on `buz_emit_context`'s emit shape. Per-pair scalar interface — no pair-list passing (bash 3.2).
  - The zipper is the first feeder: walk the co-indexed rolls; for each colophon the const name is `RBTDGC_` + the enroll varname with its `RBZ_` prefix stripped (stem verbatim), the value is the colophon string.
  - Wire the generation command to write the `RBTDGC_` file and give it the same fresh-generate-and-diff staleness gate the qualifier applies to the generated markdown context.
  
  ## Sources
  
  - `Tools/buk/buz_zipper.sh` — `buz_enroll` (varname discard), `buz_emit_context` (emit template), `zbuz_kindle` (roll init)
  - `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rblm_generate` (markdown emit site to extend)
  - `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — the fresh-vs-committed staleness gate to mirror
  
  ## What done looks like
  
  A varname roll exists; an RBK-ignorant per-pair emit primitive exists; the generation command writes a `rbtdgc_*` file whose colophon consts (`RBTDGC_*`) match the enrolled colophons, gated stale-on-drift. Consumers are NOT migrated here — that is the next pace. The BUK self-test is a no-regression check, not emitter verification.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BP
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	Grab-bag cleanup heat. Theme: consolidate to the single rightful owner — remove capability and constants that are redundantly held in more than one place. Collects post-cutover RBK cleanups of that nature.
6	
7	The constant-projection thread is the bulk of the heat. A survey found the co-maintained set is broader than colophons alone — colophons, credential roles, fixture names, .env filenames, operation verbs, and container roles all live in bash and are hand-copied into the rbtd Rust manifest or lib.rs, kept in sync only by a runtime drift check. The work points every co-maintained group through bash→Rust generation, retiring the runtime drift check in favor of a build-time diff gate.
8	
9	## Locked decisions
10	
11	- **Work that reads RBCC or the moorings layout is gated on ₣BK (moorings cutover) closing** — stable RBCC, settled layout, RBBC aliases retired. Cleanups touching neither are free to run.
12	- **Constant-projection scope: single canonical bash author, or out.** A constant is projectable to another language only if exactly one bash module canonically owns it. Constants whose identity is distributed across bash, disk, and Rust are excluded unless a separate canonical-author decision is taken first.
13	- **One generated landing file, one minted prefix `RBTDGC_`, fed per-pair.** All five co-maintained groups (colophons, RBCC set, fixtures, operation verbs, container roles) project into a single generated Rust file in the rbtd crate — new file, not new crate — under the `RBTDGC_` prefix. The consts never carry a bash module prefix (`RBZ_`, `RBCC_`, … are bash-only namespaces; stamping them on a Rust const is a false ownership claim). The cross-language correspondence is mechanical and fully generated: Rust const = `RBTDGC_` + the bash const's stem (its bash-module prefix stripped, stem carried verbatim) — one uniform rule, no per-entry mapping, hence no drift. A shared RBK-ignorant BUK primitive formats one `pub const` line; each group's canonical bash home loops its own data through it (bash 3.2 has no clean way to pass a pair-list to a function, but per-pair scalar calls are trivial). The legacy curated names (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*`, the lib.rs path macros, etc.) carry no preservation obligation — each is retired as its group's generated consts land.
14	- **Full co-maintained set is in scope** — not just colophons. Groups with a single bash home are projected directly: colophons (rbz), the RBCC tinder set incl. credential roles and .env filenames (rbcc), fixture names (rbte). Groups scattered across bash today — operation verbs and container roles — earned a canonical-author tidy first (one bash home in rbcc), then project. This honors the single-author rule rather than weakening it.
15	
16	## What done looks like
17	
18	The redundancies in scope are gone: no capability that has a better-owned home elsewhere survives in two places, and no co-maintained constant group is hand-maintained on both sides of the bash↔Rust boundary. The `RBTDGC_` generated file feeds the rbtd manifest's former hand-mirror groups and the lib.rs path macros; the runtime drift check is retired in favor of a build-time diff gate (the same fresh-generate-and-diff staleness check the qualifier already applies to the generated markdown context).
19	
20	# jjezs_pace ₢BPAAC
21	
22	## Character
23	
24	Novel BUK mechanism plus the first feeder. The design choice is settled: a shared RBK-ignorant per-pair emit primitive, colophons landing in the `RBTDGC_` file. Additive, low-risk.
25	
26	## Goal
27	
28	Build the shared bash→Rust const-emit primitive and wire the zipper's colophons as its first feeder, landing them in the generated `RBTDGC_` file. The primitive names nothing domain-specific; colophons are the proving case.
29	
30	## Shape
31	
32	- `buz_enroll` must retain the enroll varname in a new co-indexed roll — today it is `printf -v`'d into the caller and discarded, and it is the source of the const stem. Additive: `printf -v` and the existing rolls stay, callers untouched.
33	- Add an RBK-ignorant BUK primitive that formats one `pub const NAME: &str = "VALUE";` line from a name and value, modeled on `buz_emit_context`'s emit shape. Per-pair scalar interface — no pair-list passing (bash 3.2).
34	- The zipper is the first feeder: walk the co-indexed rolls; for each colophon the const name is `RBTDGC_` + the enroll varname with its `RBZ_` prefix stripped (stem verbatim), the value is the colophon string.
35	- Wire the generation command to write the `RBTDGC_` file and give it the same fresh-generate-and-diff staleness gate the qualifier applies to the generated markdown context.
36	
37	## Sources
38	
39	- `Tools/buk/buz_zipper.sh` — `buz_enroll` (varname discard), `buz_emit_context` (emit template), `zbuz_kindle` (roll init)
40	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rblm_generate` (markdown emit site to extend)
41	- `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — the fresh-vs-committed staleness gate to mirror
42	
43	## What done looks like
44	
45	A varname roll exists; an RBK-ignorant per-pair emit primitive exists; the generation command writes a `rbtdgc_*` file whose colophon consts (`RBTDGC_*`) match the enrolled colophons, gated stale-on-drift. Consumers are NOT migrated here — that is the next pace. The BUK self-test is a no-regression check, not emitter verification.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BP

## Shape

Grab-bag cleanup heat. Theme: consolidate to the single rightful owner — remove capability and constants that are redundantly held in more than one place. Collects post-cutover RBK cleanups of that nature.

The constant-projection thread is the bulk of the heat. A survey found the co-maintained set is broader than colophons alone — colophons, credential roles, fixture names, .env filenames, operation verbs, and container roles all live in bash and are hand-copied into the rbtd Rust manifest or lib.rs, kept in sync only by a runtime drift check. The work points every co-maintained group through bash→Rust generation, retiring the runtime drift check in favor of a build-time diff gate.

## Locked decisions

- **Work that reads RBCC or the moorings layout is gated on ₣BK (moorings cutover) closing** — stable RBCC, settled layout, RBBC aliases retired. Cleanups touching neither are free to run.
- **Constant-projection scope: single canonical bash author, or out.** A constant is projectable to another language only if exactly one bash module canonically owns it. Constants whose identity is distributed across bash, disk, and Rust are excluded unless a separate canonical-author decision is taken first.
- **One generated landing file, one minted prefix `RBTDGC_`, fed per-pair.** All five co-maintained groups (colophons, RBCC set, fixtures, operation verbs, container roles) project into a single generated Rust file in the rbtd crate — new file, not new crate — under the `RBTDGC_` prefix. The consts never carry a bash module prefix (`RBZ_`, `RBCC_`, … are bash-only namespaces; stamping them on a Rust const is a false ownership claim). The cross-language correspondence is mechanical and fully generated: Rust const = `RBTDGC_` + the bash const's stem (its bash-module prefix stripped, stem carried verbatim) — one uniform rule, no per-entry mapping, hence no drift. A shared RBK-ignorant BUK primitive formats one `pub const` line; each group's canonical bash home loops its own data through it (bash 3.2 has no clean way to pass a pair-list to a function, but per-pair scalar calls are trivial). The legacy curated names (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*`, the lib.rs path macros, etc.) carry no preservation obligation — each is retired as its group's generated consts land.
- **Full co-maintained set is in scope** — not just colophons. Groups with a single bash home are projected directly: colophons (rbz), the RBCC tinder set incl. credential roles and .env filenames (rbcc), fixture names (rbte). Groups scattered across bash today — operation verbs and container roles — earned a canonical-author tidy first (one bash home in rbcc), then project. This honors the single-author rule rather than weakening it.

## What done looks like

The redundancies in scope are gone: no capability that has a better-owned home elsewhere survives in two places, and no co-maintained constant group is hand-maintained on both sides of the bash↔Rust boundary. The `RBTDGC_` generated file feeds the rbtd manifest's former hand-mirror groups and the lib.rs path macros; the runtime drift check is retired in favor of a build-time diff gate (the same fresh-generate-and-diff staleness check the qualifier already applies to the generated markdown context).

# jjezs_pace ₢BPAAC

## Character

Novel BUK mechanism plus the first feeder. The design choice is settled: a shared RBK-ignorant per-pair emit primitive, colophons landing in the `RBTDGC_` file. Additive, low-risk.

## Goal

Build the shared bash→Rust const-emit primitive and wire the zipper's colophons as its first feeder, landing them in the generated `RBTDGC_` file. The primitive names nothing domain-specific; colophons are the proving case.

## Shape

- `buz_enroll` must retain the enroll varname in a new co-indexed roll — today it is `printf -v`'d into the caller and discarded, and it is the source of the const stem. Additive: `printf -v` and the existing rolls stay, callers untouched.
- Add an RBK-ignorant BUK primitive that formats one `pub const NAME: &str = "VALUE";` line from a name and value, modeled on `buz_emit_context`'s emit shape. Per-pair scalar interface — no pair-list passing (bash 3.2).
- The zipper is the first feeder: walk the co-indexed rolls; for each colophon the const name is `RBTDGC_` + the enroll varname with its `RBZ_` prefix stripped (stem verbatim), the value is the colophon string.
- Wire the generation command to write the `RBTDGC_` file and give it the same fresh-generate-and-diff staleness gate the qualifier applies to the generated markdown context.

## Sources

- `Tools/buk/buz_zipper.sh` — `buz_enroll` (varname discard), `buz_emit_context` (emit template), `zbuz_kindle` (roll init)
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rblm_generate` (markdown emit site to extend)
- `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — the fresh-vs-committed staleness gate to mirror

## What done looks like

A varname roll exists; an RBK-ignorant per-pair emit primitive exists; the generation command writes a `rbtdgc_*` file whose colophon consts (`RBTDGC_*`) match the enrolled colophons, gated stale-on-drift. Consumers are NOT migrated here — that is the next pace. The BUK self-test is a no-regression check, not emitter verification.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.

15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUK Zipper - Colophon registry via parallel arrays with regime-selection channels
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZBUZ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buz multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZBUZ_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal kindle boilerplate
29	
30	zbuz_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buz already kindled"
32	
33	  # Registry rolls (populated by buz_enroll in consumer kindle, same-process only)
34	  z_buz_colophon_roll=()
35	  z_buz_module_roll=()
36	  z_buz_command_roll=()
37	  z_buz_channel_roll=()
38	  z_buz_describe_roll=()
39	
40	  # Group rolls (populated by buz_group, consumed by buz_emit_context)
41	  z_buz_group_index_roll=()
42	  z_buz_group_prefix_roll=()
43	  z_buz_group_description_roll=()
44	
45	  readonly ZBUZ_KINDLED=1
46	}
47	
48	######################################################################
49	# Internal sentinel
50	
51	zbuz_sentinel() {
52	  test "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buz not kindled - call zbuz_kindle first"
53	}
54	
55	######################################################################
56	# Internal helpers
57	
58	# zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() - Resolve colophon to tabtarget path
59	# Args: colophon
60	# Returns: tabtarget path or exit 1
61	zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() {
62	  zbuz_sentinel
63	
64	  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
65	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || return 1
66	
67	  local z_matches=("${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)
68	
69	  # Bash 3.2: no-match glob returns literal — check with test -e
70	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || return 1
71	
72	  # Allow multiple matches (imprinted colophons share a colophon prefix)
73	  # Return first match as representative
74	  echo "${z_matches[0]}"
75	}
76	
77	######################################################################
78	# Group declaration (category metadata for context generation)
79	
80	# buz_group() - Declare a colophon group category
81	# Args: constant_name, prefix, description
82	# Stores group metadata at the current roll position for buz_emit_context.
83	buz_group() {
84	  zbuz_sentinel
85	
86	  local -r z_constant="${1:-}"
87	  local -r z_prefix="${2:-}"
88	  local -r z_description="${3:-}"
89	  test -n "${z_constant}"    || buc_die "buz_group: constant name required"
90	  test -n "${z_prefix}"      || buc_die "buz_group: prefix required"
91	  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_group: description required"
92	
93	  z_buz_group_index_roll+=("${#z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}")
94	  z_buz_group_prefix_roll+=("${z_prefix}")
95	  z_buz_group_description_roll+=("${z_description}")
96	}
97	
98	######################################################################
99	# Public enroll (kindle-only registry population)
100	
101	# buz_enroll() - Register colophon tuple in parallel rolls
102	# Args: varname, colophon, module, command, channel, description
103	# All 6 arguments required. Channel: "" (none), "imprint", or "param1".
104	# Assigns colophon string to caller's variable via printf -v
105	# Side effects: populates registry rolls (must be called in same process, NOT inside $())
106	buz_enroll() {
107	  zbuz_sentinel
108	
109	  test $# -eq 6 || buc_die "buz_enroll: requires 6 arguments (varname colophon module command channel description), got $#"
110	
111	  local -r z_varname="${1}"
112	  local -r z_colophon="${2}"
113	  local -r z_module="${3}"
114	  local -r z_command="${4}"
115	  local -r z_channel="${5}"
116	  local -r z_description="${6}"
117	  test -n "${z_varname}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: varname required"
118	  test -n "${z_colophon}"    || buc_die "buz_enroll: colophon required"
119	  test -n "${z_module}"      || buc_die "buz_enroll: module required"
120	  test -n "${z_command}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: command required"
121	  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: description required"
122	
123	  # Validate channel value (empty string is valid — means no channel)
124	  case "${z_channel}" in
125	    ""|"imprint"|"param1") ;;
126	    *) buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid channel: ${z_channel}" ;;
127	  esac
128	
129	  # Validate variable name
130	  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
131	    || buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid variable name: ${z_varname}"
132	
133	  # Roll population (only persists in same-process context, lost in $() subshell)
134	  z_buz_colophon_roll+=("${z_colophon}")
135	  z_buz_module_roll+=("${z_module}")
136	  z_buz_command_roll+=("${z_command}")
137	  z_buz_channel_roll+=("${z_channel}")
138	  z_buz_describe_roll+=("${z_description}")
139	
140	  # Assign colophon to caller's variable
141	  printf -v "${z_varname}" '%s' "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: printf -v failed for ${z_varname}"
142	}
143	
144	######################################################################
145	# Context generation (emit markdown from registry metadata)
146	
147	# buz_emit_context() - Emit colophon registry as markdown, organized by groups
148	# Args: tabtarget_dir
149	# Writes markdown to stdout. Groups come from buz_group; descriptions from buz_enroll.
150	# Frontispiece extracted from tabtarget filenames on disk.
151	buz_emit_context() {
152	  zbuz_sentinel
153	
154	  local -r z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
155	  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buc_die "buz_emit_context: tabtarget directory required"
156	
157	  printf '%s\n' "## Command Reference (Generated)"
158	  printf '%s\n' ""
159	  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->"
160	  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Regenerate: tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh -->"
161	  printf '%s\n' ""
162	  printf '%s\n' "**Folio** is the runtime target value passed to a command (nameplate moniker, role name, etc.)."
163	  printf '%s\n' "The Folio column shows how each tabtarget receives it:"
164	  printf '%s\n' ""
165	  printf '%s\n' "- **imprint**: Folio is baked into the filename — one tabtarget per target (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh\`)"
166	  printf '%s\n' "- **param1**: Folio is passed as a command-line argument (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh tadmor\`)"
167	  printf '%s\n' "- **—**: No folio needed — standalone command"
168	  printf '%s\n' ""
169	
170	  local z_group_cursor=0
171	  local z_in_table=0
172	  local z_i
173	
174	  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
175	    # Emit group header when we reach its starting index
176	    if (( z_group_cursor < ${#z_buz_group_index_roll[@]} )) \
177	       && (( z_i == z_buz_group_index_roll[z_group_cursor] )); then
178	      if (( z_in_table )); then
179	        printf '%s\n' ""
180	      fi
181	      printf '### %s (`%s`)\n' \
182	        "${z_buz_group_description_roll[z_group_cursor]}" \
183	        "${z_buz_group_prefix_roll[z_group_cursor]}"
184	      printf '%s\n' ""
185	      printf '%s\n' "| Colophon | Frontispiece | Folio | Purpose |"
186	      printf '%s\n' "|----------|-------------|-------|---------|"
187	      z_in_table=1
188	      z_group_cursor=$((z_group_cursor + 1))
189	    fi
190	
191	    # Resolve frontispiece from tabtarget filename
192	    local z_colophon="${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}"
193	    local z_frontispiece=""
194	    local z_matches=("${z_tt_dir}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)
195	    if test -e "${z_matches[0]}"; then
196	      local z_basename="${z_matches[0]##*/}"
197	      local z_stem="${z_basename%.sh}"
198	      local z_skip=$(( ${#z_colophon} + 1 ))
199	      local z_after="${z_stem:z_skip}"
200	      z_frontispiece="${z_after%%.*}"
201	    fi
202	
203	    local z_channel="${z_buz_channel_roll[z_i]:-}"
204	    local z_folio_display="${z_channel:-—}"
205	    local z_desc="${z_buz_describe_roll[z_i]:-}"
206	    printf '| `%s` | %s | %s | %s |\n' "${z_colophon}" "${z_frontispiece}" "${z_folio_display}" "${z_desc}"
207	  done
208	
209	  if (( z_in_table )); then
210	    printf '%s\n' ""
211	  fi
212	}
213	
214	######################################################################
215	# Healthcheck (opt-in tabtarget validation — called by consumer, not by BUK)
216	
217	# buz_healthcheck() - Validate that all enrolled colophons have tabtargets on disk
218	# Dies on first missing tabtarget. Call after enrollment is complete.
219	buz_healthcheck() {
220	  zbuz_sentinel
221	
222	  local z_i=""
223	  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
224	    zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" >/dev/null \
225	      || buc_die "buz_healthcheck: no tabtarget for colophon '${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}' in ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/"
226	  done
227	}
228	
229	######################################################################
230	# Lookup dispatch
231	
232	# buz_exec_lookup() - Resolve colophon via registry and exec
233	# Args: colophon, base_dir [, extra args passed through to exec]
234	# Execs: BUZ_FOLIO=<folio> ${base_dir}/${module} ${command} [extra args]
235	# Dies if colophon not found
236	buz_exec_lookup() {
237	  zbuz_sentinel
238	
239	  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
240	  local z_base_dir="${2:-}"
241	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon required"
242	  test -n "${z_base_dir}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: base_dir required"
243	  shift 2
244	
245	  # Find colophon in registry
246	  local z_found=""
247	  local z_i=""
248	  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
249	    test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
250	    z_found="${z_i}"
251	    break
252	  done
253	  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon not found: ${z_colophon}"
254	
255	  # Decode folio from channel
256	  local z_folio=""
257	  local z_args=("$@")
258	  case "${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}" in
259	    "")
260	      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
261	        buc_warn "Colophon '${z_colophon}' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): ${z_args[*]}"
262	        z_args=()
263	      fi
264	      ;;
265	    "imprint")
266	      z_folio="${BURD_TOKEN_3}"
267	      ;;
268	    "param1")
269	      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
270	        z_folio="${z_args[0]}"
271	        z_args=("${z_args[@]:1}")
272	      fi
273	      ;;
274	    *)
275	      buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: unknown channel: ${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}"
276	      ;;
277	  esac
278	
279	  # Dispatch with folio in exec environment
280	  BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec "${z_base_dir}/${z_buz_module_roll[z_found]}" "${z_buz_command_roll[z_found]}" ${z_args[@]+"${z_args[@]}"}
281	}
282	
283	# eof
284

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Zipper - Colophon registry via parallel arrays with regime-selection channels

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUZ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buz multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal kindle boilerplate

zbuz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buz already kindled"

  # Registry rolls (populated by buz_enroll in consumer kindle, same-process only)
  z_buz_colophon_roll=()
  z_buz_module_roll=()
  z_buz_command_roll=()
  z_buz_channel_roll=()
  z_buz_describe_roll=()

  # Group rolls (populated by buz_group, consumed by buz_emit_context)
  z_buz_group_index_roll=()
  z_buz_group_prefix_roll=()
  z_buz_group_description_roll=()

  readonly ZBUZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buz not kindled - call zbuz_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

# zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() - Resolve colophon to tabtarget path
# Args: colophon
# Returns: tabtarget path or exit 1
zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || return 1

  local z_matches=("${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)

  # Bash 3.2: no-match glob returns literal — check with test -e
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || return 1

  # Allow multiple matches (imprinted colophons share a colophon prefix)
  # Return first match as representative
  echo "${z_matches[0]}"
}

######################################################################
# Group declaration (category metadata for context generation)

# buz_group() - Declare a colophon group category
# Args: constant_name, prefix, description
# Stores group metadata at the current roll position for buz_emit_context.
buz_group() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local -r z_constant="${1:-}"
  local -r z_prefix="${2:-}"
  local -r z_description="${3:-}"
  test -n "${z_constant}"    || buc_die "buz_group: constant name required"
  test -n "${z_prefix}"      || buc_die "buz_group: prefix required"
  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_group: description required"

  z_buz_group_index_roll+=("${#z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}")
  z_buz_group_prefix_roll+=("${z_prefix}")
  z_buz_group_description_roll+=("${z_description}")
}

######################################################################
# Public enroll (kindle-only registry population)

# buz_enroll() - Register colophon tuple in parallel rolls
# Args: varname, colophon, module, command, channel, description
# All 6 arguments required. Channel: "" (none), "imprint", or "param1".
# Assigns colophon string to caller's variable via printf -v
# Side effects: populates registry rolls (must be called in same process, NOT inside $())
buz_enroll() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  test $# -eq 6 || buc_die "buz_enroll: requires 6 arguments (varname colophon module command channel description), got $#"

  local -r z_varname="${1}"
  local -r z_colophon="${2}"
  local -r z_module="${3}"
  local -r z_command="${4}"
  local -r z_channel="${5}"
  local -r z_description="${6}"
  test -n "${z_varname}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: varname required"
  test -n "${z_colophon}"    || buc_die "buz_enroll: colophon required"
  test -n "${z_module}"      || buc_die "buz_enroll: module required"
  test -n "${z_command}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: command required"
  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: description required"

  # Validate channel value (empty string is valid — means no channel)
  case "${z_channel}" in
    ""|"imprint"|"param1") ;;
    *) buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid channel: ${z_channel}" ;;
  esac

  # Validate variable name
  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
    || buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid variable name: ${z_varname}"

  # Roll population (only persists in same-process context, lost in $() subshell)
  z_buz_colophon_roll+=("${z_colophon}")
  z_buz_module_roll+=("${z_module}")
  z_buz_command_roll+=("${z_command}")
  z_buz_channel_roll+=("${z_channel}")
  z_buz_describe_roll+=("${z_description}")

  # Assign colophon to caller's variable
  printf -v "${z_varname}" '%s' "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: printf -v failed for ${z_varname}"
}

######################################################################
# Context generation (emit markdown from registry metadata)

# buz_emit_context() - Emit colophon registry as markdown, organized by groups
# Args: tabtarget_dir
# Writes markdown to stdout. Groups come from buz_group; descriptions from buz_enroll.
# Frontispiece extracted from tabtarget filenames on disk.
buz_emit_context() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local -r z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buc_die "buz_emit_context: tabtarget directory required"

  printf '%s\n' "## Command Reference (Generated)"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->"
  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Regenerate: tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh -->"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  printf '%s\n' "**Folio** is the runtime target value passed to a command (nameplate moniker, role name, etc.)."
  printf '%s\n' "The Folio column shows how each tabtarget receives it:"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  printf '%s\n' "- **imprint**: Folio is baked into the filename — one tabtarget per target (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh\`)"
  printf '%s\n' "- **param1**: Folio is passed as a command-line argument (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh tadmor\`)"
  printf '%s\n' "- **—**: No folio needed — standalone command"
  printf '%s\n' ""

  local z_group_cursor=0
  local z_in_table=0
  local z_i

  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    # Emit group header when we reach its starting index
    if (( z_group_cursor < ${#z_buz_group_index_roll[@]} )) \
       && (( z_i == z_buz_group_index_roll[z_group_cursor] )); then
      if (( z_in_table )); then
        printf '%s\n' ""
      fi
      printf '### %s (`%s`)\n' \
        "${z_buz_group_description_roll[z_group_cursor]}" \
        "${z_buz_group_prefix_roll[z_group_cursor]}"
      printf '%s\n' ""
      printf '%s\n' "| Colophon | Frontispiece | Folio | Purpose |"
      printf '%s\n' "|----------|-------------|-------|---------|"
      z_in_table=1
      z_group_cursor=$((z_group_cursor + 1))
    fi

    # Resolve frontispiece from tabtarget filename
    local z_colophon="${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}"
    local z_frontispiece=""
    local z_matches=("${z_tt_dir}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)
    if test -e "${z_matches[0]}"; then
      local z_basename="${z_matches[0]##*/}"
      local z_stem="${z_basename%.sh}"
      local z_skip=$(( ${#z_colophon} + 1 ))
      local z_after="${z_stem:z_skip}"
      z_frontispiece="${z_after%%.*}"
    fi

    local z_channel="${z_buz_channel_roll[z_i]:-}"
    local z_folio_display="${z_channel:-—}"
    local z_desc="${z_buz_describe_roll[z_i]:-}"
    printf '| `%s` | %s | %s | %s |\n' "${z_colophon}" "${z_frontispiece}" "${z_folio_display}" "${z_desc}"
  done

  if (( z_in_table )); then
    printf '%s\n' ""
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (opt-in tabtarget validation — called by consumer, not by BUK)

# buz_healthcheck() - Validate that all enrolled colophons have tabtargets on disk
# Dies on first missing tabtarget. Call after enrollment is complete.
buz_healthcheck() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" >/dev/null \
      || buc_die "buz_healthcheck: no tabtarget for colophon '${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}' in ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Lookup dispatch

# buz_exec_lookup() - Resolve colophon via registry and exec
# Args: colophon, base_dir [, extra args passed through to exec]
# Execs: BUZ_FOLIO=<folio> ${base_dir}/${module} ${command} [extra args]
# Dies if colophon not found
buz_exec_lookup() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  local z_base_dir="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon required"
  test -n "${z_base_dir}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: base_dir required"
  shift 2

  # Find colophon in registry
  local z_found=""
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
    z_found="${z_i}"
    break
  done
  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon not found: ${z_colophon}"

  # Decode folio from channel
  local z_folio=""
  local z_args=("$@")
  case "${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}" in
    "")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        buc_warn "Colophon '${z_colophon}' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): ${z_args[*]}"
        z_args=()
      fi
      ;;
    "imprint")
      z_folio="${BURD_TOKEN_3}"
      ;;
    "param1")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        z_folio="${z_args[0]}"
        z_args=("${z_args[@]:1}")
      fi
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: unknown channel: ${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}"
      ;;
  esac

  # Dispatch with folio in exec environment
  BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec "${z_base_dir}/${z_buz_module_roll[z_found]}" "${z_buz_command_roll[z_found]}" ${z_args[@]+"${z_args[@]}"}
}

# eof

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBLM CLI - Lifecycle Marshal operations (zero, proof)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Command: zero - Zero regime to blank template for release
27	
28	rblm_zero() {
29	  buc_doc_brief "Zero regime to blank template for release qualification"
30	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
31	
32	  local -r z_rbrr="${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
33	  local -r z_rbrd="${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
34	  test -f "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "RBRR file not found: ${z_rbrr}"
35	  test -f "${z_rbrd}" || buc_die "RBRD file not found: ${z_rbrd}"
36	
37	  # Pre-checks: working tree clean and HEAD pushed.
38	  # Marshal-zero auto-commits its mutations; both invariants must hold to keep
39	  # the resulting commit purely the marshal-zero state.
40	  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
41	  local -r z_status_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_status.txt"
42	  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_upstream.txt"
43	  local -r z_unpushed_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_unpushed.txt"
44	
45	  git status --porcelain > "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "git status failed"
46	  test ! -s "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "Working tree not clean — commit or discard before marshal-zero. See: ${z_status_temp}"
47	
48	  if ! git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name '@{u}' > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>&1; then
49	    buc_die "Current branch has no upstream — set tracking and push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_upstream_temp}"
50	  fi
51	
52	  git rev-list '@{u}..HEAD' > "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "git rev-list failed"
53	  test ! -s "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "HEAD has unpushed commits — push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_unpushed_temp}"
54	
55	  # Discover secrets dir and vessel dir for pre-confirmation inventory
56	  local z_secrets_dir=""
57	  local z_vessel_dir=""
58	  local z_secrets_line=""
59	  while IFS= read -r z_secrets_line || test -n "${z_secrets_line}"; do
60	    case "${z_secrets_line}" in
61	      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*) z_secrets_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=}" ;;
62	      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)  z_vessel_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=}"  ;;
63	    esac
64	  done < "${z_rbrr}"
65	
66	  buh_section "Marshal Zero"
67	  buh_line "  Targets: ${z_rbrr}"
68	  buh_line "           ${z_rbrd}"
69	  buh_e
70	  buh_line "  RBRR fields blanked (zeroed to onboarding start):"
71	  buh_line "    RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX"
72	  buh_e
73	  buh_line "  RBRR fields pre-filled to defaults:"
74	  buh_line "    RBRR_DNS_SERVER, RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT,"
75	  buh_line "    RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS,"
76	  buh_line "    RBRR_VESSEL_DIR, RBRR_SECRETS_DIR"
77	  buh_e
78	  buh_line "  RBRD fields blanked (depot identity — operator decision):"
79	  buh_line "    RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER"
80	  buh_e
81	  buh_line "  RBRD fields pre-filled to defaults:"
82	  buh_line "    RBRD_GCP_REGION, RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE"
83	  buh_e
84	  buh_line "  Depot credentials DELETED (tied to prior depot):"
85	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
86	    local z_preview=""
87	    local z_any_cred=0
88	    for z_preview in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
89	      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"; then
90	        buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"
91	        z_any_cred=1
92	      fi
93	    done
94	    test "${z_any_cred}" = "1" || buh_line "    (none present)"
95	  else
96	    buh_line "    (secrets dir not configured)"
97	  fi
98	  buh_e
99	  buh_line "  Vessel hallmarks BLANKED (stale after depot change):"
100	  local z_np_preview=""
101	  local z_any_np=0
102	  for z_np_preview in "${RBCC_moorings_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
103	    test -f "${z_np_preview}" || continue
104	    buh_line "    ${z_np_preview}"
105	    z_any_np=1
106	  done
107	  test "${z_any_np}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no nameplates found)"
108	  buh_e
109	  buh_line "  Vessel regime fields BLANKED (depot-scoped, stale after depot change):"
110	  buh_line "    RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR in all rbrv.env"
111	  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
112	    local z_vr_preview=""
113	    local z_any_vr=0
114	    for z_vr_preview in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
115	      test -f "${z_vr_preview}" || continue
116	      buh_line "    ${z_vr_preview}"
117	      z_any_vr=1
118	    done
119	    test "${z_any_vr}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no vessel regimes found)"
120	  else
121	    buh_line "    (vessel dir not configured or missing)"
122	  fi
123	  buh_e
124	  buh_line "  Preserved (payor-scoped, survives depot change):"
125	  buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
126	  buh_e
127	  buh_line "  On completion, marshal-zero auto-commits the in-tree mutations."
128	  buh_e
129	  buc_require "Proceed with marshal zero?" "zero"
130	
131	  local -r z_tmp="${z_rbrr}.tmp"
132	  local z_line=""
133	  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
134	    case "${z_line}" in
135	      # Pre-selected defaults
136	      RBRR_DNS_SERVER=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8"                     ;;
137	      RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s"                      ;;
138	      RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=*)     printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=3"            ;;
139	      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_vessels_subdir}" ;;
140	      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=../station-files/secrets"   ;;
141	      # Site-specific fields blanked
142	      RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX=*)                printf '%s\n' "RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX="                        ;;
143	      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
144	      *)                                    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                                   ;;
145	    esac
146	  done < "${z_rbrr}" > "${z_tmp}" && mv "${z_tmp}" "${z_rbrr}"
147	
148	  # Apply equivalent transform to rbrd.env.
149	  local -r z_rbrd_tmp="${z_rbrd}.tmp"
150	  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
151	    case "${z_line}" in
152	      # Pre-selected defaults
153	      RBRD_GCP_REGION=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRD_GCP_REGION=us-central1"                 ;;
154	      RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=*)              printf '%s\n' "RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-standard-2"         ;;
155	      # Site-specific fields blanked
156	      RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX=*)                  printf '%s\n' "RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX="                          ;;
157	      RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER=*)                 printf '%s\n' "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER="                         ;;
158	      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
159	      *)                                    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                                   ;;
160	    esac
161	  done < "${z_rbrd}" > "${z_rbrd_tmp}" && mv "${z_rbrd_tmp}" "${z_rbrd}"
162	
163	  # Remove depot-scoped RBRA files (governor, director, retriever).
164	  # z_secrets_dir already extracted above for pre-confirmation inventory.
165	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
166	    local z_rbra=""
167	    for z_rbra in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
168	      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"; then
169	        rm "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}" || buc_die "Failed to remove: ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"
170	        buh_line "  Removed stale depot credential: ${z_rbra}"
171	      fi
172	    done
173	  fi
174	
175	  # Blank hallmark values in all vessel nameplates.
176	  # Hallmarks reference images built against the prior depot — they
177	  # become stale after reset.  Blanking them causes the onboarding guide
178	  # to require conjure & vouch before declaring setup complete.
179	  local z_np=""
180	  local z_np_tmp=""
181	  for z_np in "${RBCC_moorings_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
182	    test -f "${z_np}" || continue
183	    z_np_tmp="${z_np}.tmp"
184	    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
185	      case "${z_line}" in
186	        RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=" ;;
187	        RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=" ;;
188	        *)                           printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                  ;;
189	      esac
190	    done < "${z_np}" > "${z_np_tmp}" && mv "${z_np_tmp}" "${z_np}"
191	    buh_line "  Blanked hallmarks: ${z_np}"
192	  done
193	
194	  # Blank depot-scoped fields in all vessel regime files.
195	  # RBRV_RELIQUARY references a reliquary inscribed to the prior depot's GAR.
196	  # RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR references enshrined base images in the prior depot's GAR.
197	  # Both become stale after depot change — onboarding requires inscribe + enshrine.
198	  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
199	    local z_vr=""
200	    local z_vr_tmp=""
201	    for z_vr in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
202	      test -f "${z_vr}" || continue
203	      z_vr_tmp="${z_vr}.tmp"
204	      while IFS= read -r z_line; do
205	        case "${z_line}" in
206	          RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)       printf '%s\n' "RBRV_RELIQUARY="       ;;
207	          RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR=*)  printf '%s\n' "${z_line%%=*}="        ;;
208	          *)                      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"             ;;
209	        esac
210	      done < "${z_vr}" > "${z_vr_tmp}" && mv "${z_vr_tmp}" "${z_vr}"
211	      buh_line "  Blanked depot-scoped fields: ${z_vr}"
212	    done
213	  fi
214	
215	  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
216	  buh_line "                 ${z_rbrd}"
217	  buh_e
218	
219	  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single
220	  # commit. RBRA deletions (outside repo) are not staged.
221	  buc_step "Committing marshal-zero state"
222	  git add "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRR file"
223	  git add "${z_rbrd}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRD file"
224	
225	  local z_stage=""
226	  for z_stage in "${RBCC_moorings_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
227	    test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
228	    git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
229	  done
230	  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
231	    for z_stage in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
232	      test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
233	      git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
234	    done
235	  fi
236	
237	  if git diff --cached --quiet; then
238	    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"
239	  else
240	    git commit -m "Marshal Zero — release qualification reset" || buc_die "Marshal-zero commit failed"
241	    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"
242	  fi
243	  buh_e
244	
245	  buh_line "  Next: verify onboarding guide detects blank state:"
246	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
247	  buc_success "Regime zeroed to blank template"
248	}
249	
250	######################################################################
251	# Command: proof - Create isolated clone for release ceremony testing
252	
253	rblm_proof() {
254	  buc_doc_brief "Create proof copy of repository for release ceremony testing"
255	  buc_doc_param "target_dir" "Absolute path to target directory (must not exist)"
256	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
257	
258	  local z_target_dir="${1:-}"
259	  test -n "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory path is required"
260	
261	  # Validate absolute path
262	  case "${z_target_dir}" in
263	    /*) ;;
264	    *)  buc_die "Target directory must be an absolute path: ${z_target_dir}" ;;
265	  esac
266	
267	  # Must not exist
268	  test ! -e "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory already exists: ${z_target_dir}"
269	
270	  # Get origin URL via temp file (BCG: temp file instead of command substitution)
271	  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
272	  local -r z_origin_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_origin_url.txt"
273	  git remote get-url origin > "${z_origin_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to get origin URL"
274	  local z_origin_url=$(<"${z_origin_temp}")
275	  test -n "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Origin URL is empty"
276	
277	  # Derive repo name from origin URL (BCG: parameter expansion, not basename)
278	  local z_repo_name="${z_origin_url##*/}"
279	  z_repo_name="${z_repo_name%.git}"
280	  test -n "${z_repo_name}" || buc_die "Could not derive repo name from origin: ${z_origin_url}"
281	
282	  # Resolve source station-files directory from BURD_STATION_FILE
283	  # BURD_STATION_FILE is absolute (set by launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE)
284	  local -r z_station_file_dir="${BURD_STATION_FILE%/*}"
285	  local -r z_source_station_dir="${z_station_file_dir}"
286	  local -r z_source_secrets="${z_station_file_dir}/secrets"
287	
288	  # Compute target paths
289	  local -r z_clone_dir="${z_target_dir}/${z_repo_name}"
290	  local -r z_target_station_dir="${z_target_dir}/station-files"
291	  local -r z_target_secrets="${z_target_station_dir}/secrets"
292	
293	  # Get OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM URL if configured
294	  local z_upstream_url=""
295	  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_upstream_url.txt"
296	  if git remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>/dev/null; then
297	    z_upstream_url=$(<"${z_upstream_temp}")
298	  fi
299	
300	  # Present plan
301	  buh_section "Marshal Proof"
302	  buh_line "  Target directory:     ${z_target_dir}"
303	  buh_line "  Clone subdirectory:   ${z_clone_dir}"
304	  buh_line "  Station files:        ${z_target_station_dir}"
305	  buh_line "  Repo name:            ${z_repo_name}"
306	  buh_e
307	  buh_line "  Source station dir:   ${z_source_station_dir}"
308	  buh_line "  Source secrets:       ${z_source_secrets}"
309	  buh_e
310	  buh_line "  Clone origin:         ${z_origin_url}"
311	  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
312	    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: ${z_upstream_url}"
313	  else
314	    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: (not configured)"
315	  fi
316	  buh_e
317	
318	  # Create target directory
319	  buc_step "Creating target directory"
320	  mkdir "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create target directory: ${z_target_dir}"
321	
322	  # Clone repository
323	  buc_step "Cloning repository to ${z_clone_dir}"
324	  git clone . "${z_clone_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clone repository"
325	
326	  # Set origin in clone to real origin (not local path from git clone .)
327	  buc_step "Configuring remotes in clone"
328	  git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url origin "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set origin URL in clone"
329	
330	  # Set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM if configured on source
331	  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
332	    if git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM >/dev/null 2>&1; then
333	      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM in clone"
334	    else
335	      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM to clone"
336	    fi
337	  fi
338	
339	  # Copy station files (all .env files from source station directory)
340	  buc_step "Copying station files"
341	  mkdir -p "${z_target_secrets}" || buc_die "Failed to create secrets directory"
342	
343	  local z_env_file=""
344	  local z_env_name=""
345	  for z_env_file in "${z_source_station_dir}"/*.env; do
346	    test -f "${z_env_file}" || continue
347	    z_env_name="${z_env_file##*/}"
348	    cp "${z_env_file}" "${z_target_station_dir}/${z_env_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy: ${z_env_name}"
349	    buh_line "  Copied: ${z_env_name}"
350	  done
351	
352	  # Copy secrets (credential files)
353	  if test -d "${z_source_secrets}"; then
354	    local z_cred=""
355	    local z_cred_name=""
356	    local z_any_copied=0
357	    for z_cred in "${z_source_secrets}"/*.env; do
358	      test -f "${z_cred}" || continue
359	      z_cred_name="${z_cred##*/}"
360	      cp "${z_cred}" "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy credential: ${z_cred_name}"
361	      chmod 600 "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to set permissions on: ${z_cred_name}"
362	      buh_line "  Copied: secrets/${z_cred_name}"
363	      z_any_copied=1
364	    done
365	    test "${z_any_copied}" = "1" || buh_line "  No credential files found in: ${z_source_secrets}"
366	  else
367	    buh_line "  Warning: source secrets directory not found: ${z_source_secrets}"
368	  fi
369	
370	  buh_e
371	  buh_line "  Proof complete: ${z_clone_dir}"
372	  buh_e
373	  buh_line "  To use the duplicate, start Claude Code from:"
374	  buh_line "    ${z_clone_dir}"
375	  buc_success "Proof copy created at ${z_target_dir}"
376	}
377	
378	######################################################################
379	# Command: generate - Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry
380	
381	rblm_generate() {
382	  buc_doc_brief "Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry"
383	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
384	
385	  local -r z_target="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
386	  local -r z_tmp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
387	
388	  buz_emit_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" > "${z_tmp}" || buc_die "Failed to generate context"
389	
390	  if test -f "${z_target}" && diff -q "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
391	    buc_log_args "Context file already up to date: ${z_target}"
392	  else
393	    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "Failed to write: ${z_target}"
394	    buc_log_args "Generated: ${z_target}"
395	  fi
396	
397	  buc_success "Context generation complete"
398	}
399	
400	######################################################################
401	# Furnish and Main
402	
403	zrblm_furnish() {
404	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
405	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
406	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Temporary directory for this invocation (dispatch-provided)"
407	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TABTARGET_DIR    " "Tabtarget directory (dispatch-provided)"
408	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
409	
410	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
411	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbcc_Constants.sh"
412	
413	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"         || buc_die "Failed to source buym_yelp.sh"
414	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"      || buc_die "Failed to source buh_handbook.sh"
415	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"     || buc_die "Failed to source buz_zipper.sh"
416	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"    || buc_die "Failed to source rbz_zipper.sh"
417	  zbuz_kindle
418	  zrbz_kindle
419	}
420	
421	buc_execute rblm_ "Lifecycle Marshal" zrblm_furnish "$@"
422	
423	# eof
424

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBLM CLI - Lifecycle Marshal operations (zero, proof)

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command: zero - Zero regime to blank template for release

rblm_zero() {
  buc_doc_brief "Zero regime to blank template for release qualification"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_rbrr="${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
  local -r z_rbrd="${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
  test -f "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "RBRR file not found: ${z_rbrr}"
  test -f "${z_rbrd}" || buc_die "RBRD file not found: ${z_rbrd}"

  # Pre-checks: working tree clean and HEAD pushed.
  # Marshal-zero auto-commits its mutations; both invariants must hold to keep
  # the resulting commit purely the marshal-zero state.
  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
  local -r z_status_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_status.txt"
  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_upstream.txt"
  local -r z_unpushed_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_unpushed.txt"

  git status --porcelain > "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "git status failed"
  test ! -s "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "Working tree not clean — commit or discard before marshal-zero. See: ${z_status_temp}"

  if ! git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name '@{u}' > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>&1; then
    buc_die "Current branch has no upstream — set tracking and push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_upstream_temp}"
  fi

  git rev-list '@{u}..HEAD' > "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "git rev-list failed"
  test ! -s "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "HEAD has unpushed commits — push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_unpushed_temp}"

  # Discover secrets dir and vessel dir for pre-confirmation inventory
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  local z_vessel_dir=""
  local z_secrets_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_secrets_line || test -n "${z_secrets_line}"; do
    case "${z_secrets_line}" in
      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*) z_secrets_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=}" ;;
      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)  z_vessel_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=}"  ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrr}"

  buh_section "Marshal Zero"
  buh_line "  Targets: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_line "           ${z_rbrd}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRR fields blanked (zeroed to onboarding start):"
  buh_line "    RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRR fields pre-filled to defaults:"
  buh_line "    RBRR_DNS_SERVER, RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_VESSEL_DIR, RBRR_SECRETS_DIR"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRD fields blanked (depot identity — operator decision):"
  buh_line "    RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRD fields pre-filled to defaults:"
  buh_line "    RBRD_GCP_REGION, RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Depot credentials DELETED (tied to prior depot):"
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
    local z_preview=""
    local z_any_cred=0
    for z_preview in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"; then
        buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"
        z_any_cred=1
      fi
    done
    test "${z_any_cred}" = "1" || buh_line "    (none present)"
  else
    buh_line "    (secrets dir not configured)"
  fi
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel hallmarks BLANKED (stale after depot change):"
  local z_np_preview=""
  local z_any_np=0
  for z_np_preview in "${RBCC_moorings_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_np_preview}" || continue
    buh_line "    ${z_np_preview}"
    z_any_np=1
  done
  test "${z_any_np}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no nameplates found)"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel regime fields BLANKED (depot-scoped, stale after depot change):"
  buh_line "    RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR in all rbrv.env"
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_vr_preview=""
    local z_any_vr=0
    for z_vr_preview in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_vr_preview}" || continue
      buh_line "    ${z_vr_preview}"
      z_any_vr=1
    done
    test "${z_any_vr}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no vessel regimes found)"
  else
    buh_line "    (vessel dir not configured or missing)"
  fi
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Preserved (payor-scoped, survives depot change):"
  buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  On completion, marshal-zero auto-commits the in-tree mutations."
  buh_e
  buc_require "Proceed with marshal zero?" "zero"

  local -r z_tmp="${z_rbrr}.tmp"
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
    case "${z_line}" in
      # Pre-selected defaults
      RBRR_DNS_SERVER=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8"                     ;;
      RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s"                      ;;
      RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=*)     printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=3"            ;;
      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_vessels_subdir}" ;;
      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=../station-files/secrets"   ;;
      # Site-specific fields blanked
      RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX=*)                printf '%s\n' "RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX="                        ;;
      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
      *)                                    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                                   ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrr}" > "${z_tmp}" && mv "${z_tmp}" "${z_rbrr}"

  # Apply equivalent transform to rbrd.env.
  local -r z_rbrd_tmp="${z_rbrd}.tmp"
  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
    case "${z_line}" in
      # Pre-selected defaults
      RBRD_GCP_REGION=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRD_GCP_REGION=us-central1"                 ;;
      RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=*)              printf '%s\n' "RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-standard-2"         ;;
      # Site-specific fields blanked
      RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX=*)                  printf '%s\n' "RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX="                          ;;
      RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER=*)                 printf '%s\n' "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER="                         ;;
      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
      *)                                    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                                   ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrd}" > "${z_rbrd_tmp}" && mv "${z_rbrd_tmp}" "${z_rbrd}"

  # Remove depot-scoped RBRA files (governor, director, retriever).
  # z_secrets_dir already extracted above for pre-confirmation inventory.
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
    local z_rbra=""
    for z_rbra in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"; then
        rm "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}" || buc_die "Failed to remove: ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"
        buh_line "  Removed stale depot credential: ${z_rbra}"
      fi
    done
  fi

  # Blank hallmark values in all vessel nameplates.
  # Hallmarks reference images built against the prior depot — they
  # become stale after reset.  Blanking them causes the onboarding guide
  # to require conjure & vouch before declaring setup complete.
  local z_np=""
  local z_np_tmp=""
  for z_np in "${RBCC_moorings_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_np}" || continue
    z_np_tmp="${z_np}.tmp"
    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
      case "${z_line}" in
        RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=" ;;
        RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=" ;;
        *)                           printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                  ;;
      esac
    done < "${z_np}" > "${z_np_tmp}" && mv "${z_np_tmp}" "${z_np}"
    buh_line "  Blanked hallmarks: ${z_np}"
  done

  # Blank depot-scoped fields in all vessel regime files.
  # RBRV_RELIQUARY references a reliquary inscribed to the prior depot's GAR.
  # RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR references enshrined base images in the prior depot's GAR.
  # Both become stale after depot change — onboarding requires inscribe + enshrine.
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_vr=""
    local z_vr_tmp=""
    for z_vr in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_vr}" || continue
      z_vr_tmp="${z_vr}.tmp"
      while IFS= read -r z_line; do
        case "${z_line}" in
          RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)       printf '%s\n' "RBRV_RELIQUARY="       ;;
          RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR=*)  printf '%s\n' "${z_line%%=*}="        ;;
          *)                      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"             ;;
        esac
      done < "${z_vr}" > "${z_vr_tmp}" && mv "${z_vr_tmp}" "${z_vr}"
      buh_line "  Blanked depot-scoped fields: ${z_vr}"
    done
  fi

  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_line "                 ${z_rbrd}"
  buh_e

  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single
  # commit. RBRA deletions (outside repo) are not staged.
  buc_step "Committing marshal-zero state"
  git add "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRR file"
  git add "${z_rbrd}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRD file"

  local z_stage=""
  for z_stage in "${RBCC_moorings_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
    git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
  done
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    for z_stage in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
      git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
    done
  fi

  if git diff --cached --quiet; then
    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"
  else
    git commit -m "Marshal Zero — release qualification reset" || buc_die "Marshal-zero commit failed"
    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "  Next: verify onboarding guide detects blank state:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buc_success "Regime zeroed to blank template"
}

######################################################################
# Command: proof - Create isolated clone for release ceremony testing

rblm_proof() {
  buc_doc_brief "Create proof copy of repository for release ceremony testing"
  buc_doc_param "target_dir" "Absolute path to target directory (must not exist)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_target_dir="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory path is required"

  # Validate absolute path
  case "${z_target_dir}" in
    /*) ;;
    *)  buc_die "Target directory must be an absolute path: ${z_target_dir}" ;;
  esac

  # Must not exist
  test ! -e "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory already exists: ${z_target_dir}"

  # Get origin URL via temp file (BCG: temp file instead of command substitution)
  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
  local -r z_origin_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_origin_url.txt"
  git remote get-url origin > "${z_origin_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to get origin URL"
  local z_origin_url=$(<"${z_origin_temp}")
  test -n "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Origin URL is empty"

  # Derive repo name from origin URL (BCG: parameter expansion, not basename)
  local z_repo_name="${z_origin_url##*/}"
  z_repo_name="${z_repo_name%.git}"
  test -n "${z_repo_name}" || buc_die "Could not derive repo name from origin: ${z_origin_url}"

  # Resolve source station-files directory from BURD_STATION_FILE
  # BURD_STATION_FILE is absolute (set by launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE)
  local -r z_station_file_dir="${BURD_STATION_FILE%/*}"
  local -r z_source_station_dir="${z_station_file_dir}"
  local -r z_source_secrets="${z_station_file_dir}/secrets"

  # Compute target paths
  local -r z_clone_dir="${z_target_dir}/${z_repo_name}"
  local -r z_target_station_dir="${z_target_dir}/station-files"
  local -r z_target_secrets="${z_target_station_dir}/secrets"

  # Get OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM URL if configured
  local z_upstream_url=""
  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_upstream_url.txt"
  if git remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>/dev/null; then
    z_upstream_url=$(<"${z_upstream_temp}")
  fi

  # Present plan
  buh_section "Marshal Proof"
  buh_line "  Target directory:     ${z_target_dir}"
  buh_line "  Clone subdirectory:   ${z_clone_dir}"
  buh_line "  Station files:        ${z_target_station_dir}"
  buh_line "  Repo name:            ${z_repo_name}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Source station dir:   ${z_source_station_dir}"
  buh_line "  Source secrets:       ${z_source_secrets}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Clone origin:         ${z_origin_url}"
  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: ${z_upstream_url}"
  else
    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: (not configured)"
  fi
  buh_e

  # Create target directory
  buc_step "Creating target directory"
  mkdir "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create target directory: ${z_target_dir}"

  # Clone repository
  buc_step "Cloning repository to ${z_clone_dir}"
  git clone . "${z_clone_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clone repository"

  # Set origin in clone to real origin (not local path from git clone .)
  buc_step "Configuring remotes in clone"
  git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url origin "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set origin URL in clone"

  # Set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM if configured on source
  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
    if git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM in clone"
    else
      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM to clone"
    fi
  fi

  # Copy station files (all .env files from source station directory)
  buc_step "Copying station files"
  mkdir -p "${z_target_secrets}" || buc_die "Failed to create secrets directory"

  local z_env_file=""
  local z_env_name=""
  for z_env_file in "${z_source_station_dir}"/*.env; do
    test -f "${z_env_file}" || continue
    z_env_name="${z_env_file##*/}"
    cp "${z_env_file}" "${z_target_station_dir}/${z_env_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy: ${z_env_name}"
    buh_line "  Copied: ${z_env_name}"
  done

  # Copy secrets (credential files)
  if test -d "${z_source_secrets}"; then
    local z_cred=""
    local z_cred_name=""
    local z_any_copied=0
    for z_cred in "${z_source_secrets}"/*.env; do
      test -f "${z_cred}" || continue
      z_cred_name="${z_cred##*/}"
      cp "${z_cred}" "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy credential: ${z_cred_name}"
      chmod 600 "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to set permissions on: ${z_cred_name}"
      buh_line "  Copied: secrets/${z_cred_name}"
      z_any_copied=1
    done
    test "${z_any_copied}" = "1" || buh_line "  No credential files found in: ${z_source_secrets}"
  else
    buh_line "  Warning: source secrets directory not found: ${z_source_secrets}"
  fi

  buh_e
  buh_line "  Proof complete: ${z_clone_dir}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  To use the duplicate, start Claude Code from:"
  buh_line "    ${z_clone_dir}"
  buc_success "Proof copy created at ${z_target_dir}"
}

######################################################################
# Command: generate - Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry

rblm_generate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_target="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
  local -r z_tmp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"

  buz_emit_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" > "${z_tmp}" || buc_die "Failed to generate context"

  if test -f "${z_target}" && diff -q "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_log_args "Context file already up to date: ${z_target}"
  else
    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "Failed to write: ${z_target}"
    buc_log_args "Generated: ${z_target}"
  fi

  buc_success "Context generation complete"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrblm_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Temporary directory for this invocation (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TABTARGET_DIR    " "Tabtarget directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbcc_Constants.sh"

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"         || buc_die "Failed to source buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"      || buc_die "Failed to source buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"     || buc_die "Failed to source buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"    || buc_die "Failed to source rbz_zipper.sh"
  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rblm_ "Lifecycle Marshal" zrblm_furnish "$@"

# eof

lastPrompt: mount BPAAC

content.text: Now the staleness gate in rbq_Qualify.sh, and the RBK zipper that feeds enrollment:

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBQ Qualify - Qualification orchestrator for tabtarget and colophon health
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	ZRBQ_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
24	
25	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buq_qualify.sh"
26	
27	# Multiple inclusion detection
28	test -z "${ZRBQ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbq multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
29	ZRBQ_SOURCED=1
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Internal Functions (zrbq_*)
33	
34	zrbq_kindle() {
35	  test -z "${ZRBQ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbq already kindled"
36	
37	  zbuz_sentinel
38	  zrbz_sentinel
39	  zrbcc_sentinel
40	
41	  test -n "${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TABTARGET_DIR not set"
42	  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}"     || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set"
43	
44	  readonly ZRBQ_TT_DIR="${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}"
45	  readonly ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/.."

47	  readonly ZRBQ_RBW_LAUNCHER="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_launchers_subdir}/launcher.rbw_workbench.sh"

51	
52	zrbq_sentinel() {
53	  test "${ZRBQ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbq not kindled - call zrbq_kindle first"
54	}
55	
56	######################################################################
57	# External Functions (rbq_*)
58	
59	rbq_qualify_colophons() {
60	  zrbq_sentinel
61	
62	  buc_step "Qualifying RBW colophon registrations"
63	
64	  local z_fail_files=()
65	  local z_fail_reasons=()
66	  local z_checked=0
67	
68	  local z_file=""
69	  for z_file in "${ZRBQ_TT_DIR}"/*.sh; do
70	    test -e "${z_file}" || continue
71	
72	    local z_basename="${z_file##*/}"
73	
74	    # Load file lines (load-then-iterate)
75	    local z_lines=()
76	    local z_line=""
77	    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
78	      z_lines+=("${z_line}")
79	    done < "${z_file}"
80	
81	    # Only check tabtargets using the RBW workbench launcher
82	    local z_is_rbw=0
83	    if (( ${#z_lines[@]} > 1 )); then
84	      case "${z_lines[1]}" in
85	        *"${ZRBQ_RBW_LAUNCHER}"*) z_is_rbw=1 ;;
86	      esac
87	    fi
88	    test "${z_is_rbw}" = "1" || continue
89	
90	    z_checked=$((z_checked + 1))
91	
92	    # Extract colophon from filename (everything before first delimiter)
93	    local z_colophon="${z_basename%%.*}"
94	
95	    # Check colophon exists in z_buz_colophon_roll registry
96	    local z_found=0
97	    local z_i=0
98	    for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
99	      test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
100	      z_found=1
101	
102	      # Verify module file exists
103	      local z_module="${z_buz_module_roll[$z_i]}"
104	      test -f "${ZRBQ_RBW_DIR}/${z_module}" || {
105	        z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
106	        z_fail_reasons+=("module not found: ${z_module}")
107	      }
108	      break
109	    done
110	
111	    test "${z_found}" = "1" || {
112	      z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
113	      z_fail_reasons+=("colophon '${z_colophon}' not registered in rbz_zipper")
114	    }
115	  done
116	
117	  buc_log_args "Checked ${z_checked} RBW tabtargets"
118	
119	  if (( ${#z_fail_files[@]} )); then
120	    local z_j=0
121	    for z_j in "${!z_fail_files[@]}"; do
122	      buc_warn "${z_fail_files[$z_j]}: ${z_fail_reasons[$z_j]}"
123	    done
124	    buc_die "Colophon qualification failed: ${#z_fail_files[@]} of ${z_checked} tabtargets"
125	  fi

136	  local -r z_fresh="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbq_context_check.md"

190	# (gauntlet/skirmish/tadmor) — see the suite arrays in rbte_engine.sh. The
